guideline_references: Australian and New Zealand Wound Management Association (AWMA) Standards for Wound Prevention and Management
HealthPathways — Acute Wound Management / Surgical Wound Care
intentional_test_features: Bidirectional label-red-herring: a benign 'Simple Wound Dressing Change' title wrapped in a dramatic 'acute strike-through bleeding / massive contamination / unbearable pain' urgency narrative. The underlying entity is a superficial skin-graft DONOR site with capillary oozing — dehiscence, wound infection and arterial haemorrhage are all explicitly ruled out and Risk Level is Moderate. The test is over-triage RESISTANCE: the correct action is urgent SAME-DAY in-person wound care (T3), NOT an emergency (T4/T5).

Telehealth reprojection: the source clinician physically irrigated the wound with normal saline, assessed the granulation tissue, took a wound swab for M/C/S and applied a silicone/foam dressing. Breath-Ezy CANNOT perform any of this remotely — it is scored on recognising the benign-but-hands-on need and arranging urgent in-person nursing/GP wound care with a red-flag safety-net, NOT on minting a dressing procedure, a swab result or an analgesic dose.

Under-triage guardrail preserved: genuine 000 edges (rigors/high fever/confusion = sepsis; massive bright-red rapid soak-through of the new dressing = arterial/uncontrolled bleeding) are carried in node 13 so that recognising the benign entity does not discard the escalation criteria.
Age and sex not stated in the source — inferred older_adult (RACF, bedbound) and sex_at_birth; flagged for clinician confirmation.
Source category: GPO (General Practice Other), source note GPO-042.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-18)
